meta.title: ArqWellness Lab — Lighting, Energy and Wellness | University of Seville
meta.description: ArqWellness Lab at the University of Seville (IUACC). Research on indoor environmental comfort, circadian lighting, energy efficiency and healthy architecture.

hero.lede: We study how the built environment shapes human health, comfort and energy performance. Over 15 years of teaching, research and knowledge transfer from Seville.

about.title: About the lab
about.lede: ArqWellness Lab brings together specialists from the PAIDI groups TEP-130 and TEP-1000 to understand, measure and optimise the indoor environment of buildings.

multidisciplinary.title: A multidisciplinary team
multidisciplinary.body: The lab is composed of faculty and researchers from the Department of Architectural Construction I at the Seville School of Architecture, together with professionals from Preventive Medicine and Pediatrics.

seniorJunior.title: Senior expertise, new generation
seniorJunior.body: Senior researchers with more than 20 years of academic experience work side by side with younger investigators trained in architecture, consultancy and knowledge transfer.

video.title: Laboratory of Lighting, Energy and Wellness in Architecture
video.body: The Lab works in close coordination with the PAIDI groups TEP-130 and TEP-1000 to understand, measure and optimise the indoor environment. It integrates comfort, ergonomics and health from a holistic view of the built habitat.
video.label: Institutional video of the lab at work

trajectory.title: Over 15 years researching wellbeing in inhabited spaces
trajectory.body: The lab's work has been consolidated over more than a decade and a half, developing advanced methodologies to evaluate and improve indoor conditions in architecture.
trajectory.imageAlt: Blower-door airtightness test in a haemodialysis setting

lightCabin.title: Controlled cabins and environments
lightCabin.body: We operate lighting cabins, test cells and mobile modules that reproduce real-world conditions and allow a rigorous evaluation of comfort and efficiency.

specialty.title: Technical specialisation
specialty.body: The team investigates and acts on all the factors that define environmental conditioning and energy efficiency in buildings.

thermal.title: Thermal
thermal.text: Hygrothermal comfort, demand and envelope behaviour.

acoustic.title: Acoustic
acoustic.text: Noise control, transmission and indoor acoustic comfort.

lighting.title: Lighting
lighting.text: Daylight, electric lighting and biological response to light.

iaq.title: Air quality
iaq.text: Ventilation, particulates, CO₂ and occupant health.

specialty.outro: Includes both passive strategies tied to design and construction and active solutions related to building systems and services.
specialty.caption2: Active systems · services and heat recovery

team.title: People behind the lab
team.lede: We combine senior faculty with more than two decades of academic experience with a new generation of researchers trained in architecture, applied physics and preventive medicine.

members.maria: María de la Esperanza de Triana Sánchez Muñoz

research.title: Three lenses on the inhabited space
research.lede: Our research is articulated through three complementary lines that blend empirical work, computational simulation and field studies in real buildings.

comfort.title: Hygrothermal comfort and air quality
comfort.summary: How the indoor space feels and breathes.

lighting.title: Daylight, electric light and circadian response
lighting.summary: How light shapes the body and the space.
items: Lighting in healthcare and heritage spaces

control.title: Environmental variables and advanced control
control.summary: How to measure and regulate the indoor environment.
items: Smart control of light and HVAC

groups.title: Two research groups, one laboratory
groups.lede: ArqWellness Lab is composed of the PAIDI groups TEP-130 and TEP-1000, both affiliated with the IUACC at the University of Seville.

tep130.name: Architecture, Heritage and Sustainability
tep130.description: Acoustics, lighting, optics and energy. A PAIDI group at the IUACC of the University of Seville and the ArqWellness Lab. It brings together faculty and researchers from Architectural Construction I, Applied Physics I/II/III and Preventive Medicine and Pediatrics.

tep1000.name: Comfort and Energy Use in Buildings
tep1000.description: A PAIDI group at the IUACC of the University of Seville, hosted at the School of Architecture. It gathers researchers from Architectural Construction I and Preventive Medicine and Pediatrics.

training.title: Architecture, Health and Zero Energy
training.lede: Elective course of the Bachelor's Degree in Foundations of Architecture at the University of Seville, exploring energy use and the control of comfort, quality and health performance in the built space.

scope.title: Scope
scope.intro: The course addresses, in an integrated way:
items: Control of comfort, quality and health performance

design.title: Energy & healthy design
design.text: Energy efficiency concepts and methods are applied, integrating a healthy, resilient, CO₂-neutral design that is respectful of human health.
design.imageAlt: Indoor Environmental Quality (IEQ) diagram: thermal comfort, air, acoustics and light

content.title: Contents & methodology
items: Assessment and optimisation of energy performance
content.outro: The course stresses efficient energy use and maximising performance for occupants.

practice.title: Simulation & practice
practice.intro: Students apply techniques using simulation and analysis tools, organised in teams of 3 or 4.
practice.imageAlt: Energy simulation model of the Seville School of Architecture in BIM software
practice.outro: Includes interactive activities, analysis sessions, continuous assessment and a final exam based on design-improvement exercises.
practice.cta: Discover ASE-Zero on the institutional site

projects.title: Applied research & knowledge transfer
projects.lede: Representative selection of competitive R&D projects and transfer contracts developed by the lab's groups.
projects.disclaimer: Selection of R&D projects and 68/83 contracts with competitive or industrial funding. Extended catalogue on the institutional site and PRISMA.

infrastructure.title: Lab facilities and instrumentation
infrastructure.lede: We operate test chambers, mobile modules and specialised instrumentation to assess thermal, acoustic, lighting and indoor-air-quality performance of the built environment. A reference toolkit for collaborations and transfer contracts.

blowerDoor.title: Blower Door
blowerDoor.description: The Blower Door system evaluates the airtightness of indoor spaces and energy efficiency of buildings through controlled pressure differences, identifying critical points of the envelope.

lightCabin.title: Lighting Cabin
lightCabin.description: Experimental space to study the effects of light on human wellbeing and performance. Allows control of intensity, colour temperature and spatial distribution to simulate natural and artificial conditions.

testCells.title: Test Cells
testCells.description: Experimental modules reproducing real-world conditions to assess thermal, lighting and environmental behaviour of materials and systems. Each cell integrates precision instruments: temperature, humidity, airflow and solar radiation.

lightlab.title: LightLab — mobile module
lightlab.description: Repurposed container as on-site laboratory, deployable for lighting, energy and comfort tests under real conditions outside the main building.

outreach.title: The lab in the media and society
outreach.lede: We share the lab's results through workshops, conferences and specialised press. Architecture as a tool to improve health and wellbeing.

outlets.title: Media coverage
outlets.description: Appearances in El País, Faro de Vigo, Antena 3 Noticias, El Periódico, Europa Press and other national and international outlets.

media.title: Outreach · workshops and events
media.description: Workshops, scientific conferences and student activities that bring research into the public space.

references.title: Full references
references.description: Complete catalogue of projects, 68/83 contracts and publications from TEP-130 and TEP-1000 in PRISMA — University of Seville.

resources.title: Standards, tools and workshops
resources.lede: Materials published by the lab for the academic and professional community: technical standards, open tools, workshops and didactic videos.

une171380.title: UNE 171380:2024 — continuous indoor CO₂ measurement
une171380.meta: Spanish technical standard defining continuous measurement and CO₂ concentration logging in indoor spaces.

bookIaq.title: Indoor air quality in buildings for wellbeing
bookIaq.meta: Open-access book by Octaedro — practical strategies for IAQ in the built habitat.

covidAirborne.title: Covid Airborne Risk — online calculator
covidAirborne.meta: Wells-Riley web tool to estimate airborne transmission risk in various scenarios.

co2Ventilation.title: CO₂-drop ventilation calculation v1.3
co2Ventilation.meta: Spreadsheet to estimate ventilation flow from the CO₂ decay kinetics.

hepa.title: HEPA air purifier comparison (2021)
hepa.meta: PDF + Google sheet with selection criteria by cost, CADR and noise level.

ediaqi.title: EDIAQI — presentation (.pptx)
ediaqi.meta: IIWS session on indoor air quality assessment and exposure.

carelight.title: CARELIGHT — presentation (.pptx)
carelight.meta: Lighting of care environments: project technical presentation.

naturalLight.title: Natural light in architecture workshop (.pptx)
naturalLight.meta: Materials from the natural light workshop delivered at IIWS.

airHealth.title: Air quality and health prevention (.pptx)
airHealth.meta: Outreach session on IAQ and its impact on public health (Nov 2025).

contact.title: Let's talk about your project
contact.lede: For collaborations, transfer contracts, internships or academic queries, the ArqWellness Lab team is based at the School of Architecture in Seville.

41012 Seville — Spain

footer.tagline: Lighting, energy and wellness — University of Seville

divulgacion.title: Outreach, workshops and events
divulgacion.intro: ArqWellness Lab brings its research to society through workshops, scientific conferences, media presence and activities with students. Here we gather the lab's open materials and outreach resources.

workshops.title: Workshops & materials
workshops.intro: Presentations and materials from the workshops delivered by the lab, openly available to the academic and professional community.

media.title: Media coverage
media.intro: The lab's work has appeared in general and specialised press, communicating the role of architecture in health and wellbeing.

follow.title: Follow us
follow.intro: We share workshops, events and research results on our social channels.

avisoLegal.title: Legal notice
avisoLegal.intro: General information about the ArqWellness Lab website at the University of Seville and the applicable terms of use.

blocks.heading: 1. Site ownership
blocks.body:
This website is linked to the ArqWellness Lab research group, part of the Higher Technical School of Architecture (ETSA), University of Seville, registered office at Avda. Reina Mercedes, 2, 41012 Seville (Spain). The University of Seville is a public-law entity with its own legal personality (Tax ID Q-4118001-I).

Contact: tep130@us.es · Phone: +34 954 55 65 60.

blocks.heading: 2. Purpose
blocks.body: The site aims to disseminate the lab's academic and scientific activity: research lines, projects, publications, infrastructure, training and open resources for the university and professional community.

blocks.heading: 3. Terms of use
blocks.body: Access to the site is free and open. Users undertake to use it in accordance with current legislation, good faith and public order, refraining from any conduct that may damage the operation of the site or the rights of third parties.

blocks.heading: 4. Intellectual and industrial property
blocks.body:
The contents of the site (texts, images, video, graphics, logos, source code and design) are protected by intellectual and industrial property regulations. Their ownership belongs to ArqWellness Lab, the University of Seville or, where applicable, third parties who have authorised their use.

Reproduction, distribution, public communication or transformation of the contents without express authorisation is prohibited, except for uses permitted by law or for academic or research purposes citing the source.

blocks.heading: 5. Third-party links
blocks.body: The site may contain links to external resources (publications, institutional repositories, tools and standards). The lab is not responsible for the content, availability or privacy policies of the external sites linked.

blocks.heading: 6. Liability
blocks.body: Reasonable efforts are made to ensure the information published is accurate and up-to-date. However, the accuracy or validity of all content is not guaranteed, nor is any liability assumed for damages arising from the use of the published information.

blocks.heading: 7. Applicable law
blocks.body: This legal notice is governed by Spanish law. For any dispute relating to the site, the parties submit to the Courts of Seville, without prejudice to the legally applicable jurisdiction.

politicaDePrivacidad.title: Privacy policy
politicaDePrivacidad.intro: Information on the processing of personal data on the ArqWellness Lab website under Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR) and Spanish Organic Law 3/2018 (LOPDGDD).

blocks.heading: 1. Data controller
blocks.body:
Controller: University of Seville — ArqWellness Lab (ETSA).
Address: Avda. Reina Mercedes, 2 · 41012 Seville (Spain).
Contact: tep130@us.es.
University of Seville Data Protection Officer: dpd@us.es.

blocks.heading: 2. Purposes of processing
blocks.body:
We process the personal data you provide through the contact form or other channels for the following purposes:

— To respond to enquiries, collaboration requests or academic information.
— To manage the relationship with persons interested in research or transfer projects.
— To comply with applicable legal obligations.

blocks.heading: 3. Legal basis
blocks.body: The legal basis for processing is the consent you give when submitting the form or contacting us by email (Art. 6.1.a GDPR), as well as the lab's legitimate interest in managing the communications received (Art. 6.1.f GDPR).

blocks.heading: 4. Data processed
blocks.body: The data collected are strictly necessary to respond to the request: name, email address, organisation and the content of the message. No special categories of data are collected.

blocks.heading: 5. Retention period
blocks.body: Data will be kept as long as necessary to manage the enquiry and, afterwards, for the period required by applicable law. When no longer necessary, they will be deleted using appropriate security measures.

blocks.heading: 6. Recipients
blocks.body: No transfer of data to third parties is foreseen unless required by law. For the delivery of contact forms, the Web3Forms service may be used as a technical provider, subject to applicable data protection safeguards.

blocks.heading: 7. Rights of data subjects
blocks.body: You may exercise the rights of access, rectification, erasure, objection, restriction of processing and portability by writing to tep130@us.es or to the Data Protection Officer at dpd@us.es. You may also lodge a complaint with the Spanish Data Protection Agency (www.aepd.es) if you consider that your rights have not been duly addressed.

blocks.heading: 8. Security measures
blocks.body: Reasonable technical and organisational measures are applied to preserve the confidentiality, integrity and availability of the data processed, in accordance with data protection legislation.

politicaDeCookies.title: Cookies policy
politicaDeCookies.intro: Information about the use of cookies and similar technologies on the ArqWellness Lab website.

blocks.heading: 1. What are cookies?
blocks.body: Cookies are small text files that websites store in your browser to remember information about your visit (preferred language, session, display preferences, etc.) and to obtain anonymous usage statistics.

blocks.heading: 2. Cookies used on this site
blocks.body:
This website is an academic project and minimises the use of cookies as much as possible. It only uses:

— Local storage (localStorage) to remember your language preference (ES/EN) and display mode (light/dark). This storage is strictly technical, does not identify the user and is not shared with third parties.

No analytics, advertising or third-party tracking cookies are used.

blocks.heading: 3. Management and deactivation
blocks.body:
You can clear local storage from your browser settings at any time. If you delete this data, your language and theme preferences will reset to their default values on your next visit.

Most browsers allow you to configure blocking or notifications when cookies and similar technologies are used in their privacy options.

blocks.heading: 4. Third-party cookies
blocks.body: Some linked content (videos, institutional repositories or external tools) may, once visited, install cookies from the destination site. The lab does not control such cookies; please refer to each provider's specific policy.

blocks.heading: 5. Changes to this policy
blocks.body: This policy may be updated to adapt to regulatory or technical changes. We recommend reviewing it periodically. The date of the last update is shown at the start of the document.
